# Global top-N ranking with windowcomp row_number() ordered by a preceding sort

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

An un-partitioned ranking across the whole result takes its order from a PRECEDING `| sort`, not an inline one (`windowcomp row_number() sort desc x` is rejected). Sort first, then `windowcomp row_number() as rnk` numbers the rows. Use `row_number()` — `rank()` 500-errors on this tenant. This is the XQL equivalent of SQL `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ...)`.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| comp sum(bytes_out) as out_by_user by username
| sort desc out_by_user
| windowcomp row_number() as rnk
| fields rnk, username, out_by_user
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
